channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Here's when 2025 Election Day early in-person voting comes to a close across the US
item.description: Early voting deadlines approach fast across America with major races in New Jersey, Virginia and New York City. Find your state's cutoff date before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protesters gather as Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits Minnesota for ICE briefing
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem visited Minnesota Friday to discuss federal immigration enforcement efforts in Minnesota. Protests took place outside the event.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, Jon Collins, and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Texas’ congressional delegation wants Trump to punish Mexico for missing key water deadline
item.description: The state’s citrus industry is at risk, farmers say, after Mexico failed to deliver water it owes Texas as part of a 1944 treaty.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump heads to 3-country sprint in Asia, meeting with Xi as government shutdown drags on at home
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump headed for Asia Friday night for the first time this term, a trip where he’s expected to work on investment deals and peace efforts before meeting face-to-face with Chinese President Xi Jinping to try to deescalate a trade war.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARK SCHIEFELBEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration won't tap contingency fund to keep food aid flowing, memo says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is rejecting the idea of using roughly $5 billion in contingency funds to keep food aid flowing into November amid the government shutdown, according to a Department of Agriculture memo that surfaced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeffries backs Mamdani for NYC mayor months after primary
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries on Friday endorsed Zohran Mamdani for mayor of New York City, saying he respected the will of the primary voters and young people inspired by the Democratic socialist state lawmaker's candidacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to jet off to Asia as North Korea fires ballistic missiles and China trade questions loom
item.description: Trump's Asia summit tour includes meetings with Chinese President Xi Jinping and other regional leaders as trade tensions with Beijing escalate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says a Canadian ad misstated Ronald Reagan's views on tariffs. Here are the facts and context
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pulled out of trade talks with Canada Thursday night, furious over what he called a “fake’’ television ad from Ontario’s provincial government that quoted former U.S.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bioasphalt could be the savior of soybean farmers
item.description: Soybean farmers caught in the middle of a trade war could find a new market for their crops in the form of bioasphalt.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's trade war with Canada: A timeline of how we got here
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's barrage of tariffs has quickly strained the United States' longstanding relationship with Canada over the last year. Since taking office in January, Trump has threatened Canada's economy and sovereignty with steep import taxes on its goods.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House responds to reports Trump named new ballroom after himself
item.description: The White House told Fox News Digital that any announcement on a name for President Trump's White House ballroom will come directly from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cruz 'cannot support' Trump's Kuwait ambassador pick over Muslim Brotherhood remarks
item.description: Hamtramck Mayor Amer Ghalib faced bipartisan opposition during a contentious confirmation hearing for the Kuwait ambassador role over controversial social media posts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ongoing shutdown threatens food aid for 42 million
item.description: SNAP, the country's largest anti-hunger program, dates back to the Great Depression and has never been disrupted this way. Most recipients are seniors, families with kids, and those with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden, MPR News

item.title: New York Attorney General Letitia James pleads not guilty in mortgage fraud case pushed by Trump
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James accused the Trump administration of using the justice system as a “tool of revenge” after she pleaded not guilty Friday in a federal mortgage fraud case the president pressed the Justice Department to bring.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday Special: Rochester's growth spurt is driving economic, political change
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst hosts a special broadcast of Politics Friday from Rochester, Minn., exploring how the city’s makeover plays into the regional hub’s economic and political standing.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump speaks at tele-rally to boost GOP's chances in governor's race in New Jersey
item.description: President Donald Trump is urging his supporters in New Jersey to vote for Republican gubernatorial candidate Jack Ciattarelli.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: It’s supposed to be payday for many federal workers. Instead, they’re getting nothing
item.description: Roughly 1.4 million federal workers are going without pay due to the government shutdown. About half of them are furloughed, while the other half has been deemed essential and is working without pay.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Abamu and Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: SNAP pause could harm families, say county workers
item.description: Minnesota officials say residents who get food assistance won’t receive their benefits for November, as long as the federal shutdown continues. County workers say they’re now hearing from concerned enrollees in the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Inflation hits 3% in first report since shutdown
item.description: The data comes more than a week late, after the Bureau of Labor called back furloughed employees to finish it.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump ends trade talks with Canada over tariffs ad that Ontario premier now says he'll phase out
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford says he’s pulling the TV ad that caused President Donald Trump to say he was ending trade negotiations with Canada.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US military flew supersonic B-1 bombers up to the coast of Venezuela
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military flew a pair of supersonic, heavy bombers up to the coast of Venezuela on Thursday, a little over a week after another group of American bombers made a similar journey as part of a training exercise to simulate an attack. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams endorses Andrew Cuomo
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed independent candidate former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo as his successor on Thursday. During Wednesday night’s final mayoral debate, Cuomo was the only candidate who said he’d accept an endorsement from Adams when asked by moderators.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: 53 percent disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House to make room for his $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stephen A. Smith apologizes to Crockett over 'street verbiage' remark
item.description: Pundit Stephen A. Smith apologized this week for comments he made criticizing Rep. Jasmine Crockett (D-Texas) and her approach to politics.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Pritzker calls for 'truth, justice' in response to Trump takeover
item.description: "I am angry because this not the country that I want for us," Gov. J.B. Pritzker said while announcing the creation of the Illinois Accountability Commission.
item.dc:creator: Meg Hilling, NewsNation

item.title: Farmers get long-awaited relief as USDA reopens field offices
item.description: The Department of Agriculture is reopening thousands of field offices to help distribute billions of dollars in critical aid.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the final New York City mayor's debate
item.description: There was no shortage of fireworks between Democrat Zohran Mamdani, independent Andrew Cuomo and Republican Curtis Sliwa.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: Texans split on Trump's immigration policies
item.description: A new poll released Wednesday finds registered voters in Texas are split on immigration policies implemented under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats block government funding bill for 12th time after marathon floor protest
item.description: Senate Democrats voted Wednesday for a 12th time to block a House-passed bill to reopen the government and keep it funded through Nov. 21. Democrats blocked the funding measure — a clean continuing resolution that would prolong Biden-era funding levels — after liberal Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: USDA reopening 2,100 aid offices despite shutdown
item.description: The Agriculture Department will reopen about 2,100 county offices all across the country Thursday despite the ongoing government shutdown to help farmers and ranchers get access to $3 billion of aid from existing programs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge agrees to extend block on Guard deployment in Chicago while awaiting Supreme Court ruling
item.description: As the U.S. Supreme Court weighs whether to clear the way for the National Guard in Chicago, a federal judge on Wednesday agreed to extend a two-week block on Guard deployment in the Chicago area by 30 days.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Newsom deploys National Guard to support food banks amid federal shutdown
item.description: (FOX 5/KUSI) -- As the government shutdown reached its 22nd day, California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced on Wednesday he will deploy National Guard troops to support food banks throughout the state.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pick to lead watchdog agency withdraws after offensive text messages revealed
item.description: President Trump’s pick to lead a federal watchdog agency withdrew from consideration Tuesday evening after his offensive text messages were made public and GOP senators revolted.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What the federal government shutdown means countrywide
item.description: The federal government remains shut down. The NPR Network is following the ways the shutdown is affecting services across the country.
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network, MPR News

item.title: Florida lawmakers focus on affordability ahead of 2026 session
item.description: Last year was supposed to be the year of affordability, but the same concerns still linger as lawmakers head into the 2026 session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China hits the U.S. back in trade war
item.description: China has expanded its export rules on rare earths, requiring foreign firms to get approval for exporting products containing even small amounts of China-originated materials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces tariffs and an end to U.S. aid to Colombia amid clash over drug trade
item.description: The U.S. will slash assistance to Colombia and enact tariffs on its exports because the country’s leader, Gustavo Petro, “does nothing to stop” drug production, President Donald Trump said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
